title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Nicolaitis instructs Mrs. Hubbard to have a cabinet door repaired and a brighter light bulb installed in the hallway, insisting the police cover the repair costs. Mrs. Nicolaitis expresses fear that someone is following her, though Mrs. Hubbard notes her employer has a habit of exaggerating. After Mrs. Nicolaitis leaves the house and walks down Walnut Street, she enters "The Queen's Necklace" pub. While drinking brandy, she broods over the police discovering her "secret stash" and worries that Girolamo and others will spread this information. After being interrupted by an acquaintance, she leaves the pub and attempts to walk home. Feeling dizzy from the alcohol, she stumbles near a mailbox and collapses. A clerk alerts Officer Bote, who finds her on the ground and concludes she is merely drunk.

Meanwhile, Hercule Poirot examines four canvas backpacks, including one purchased from Mr. Hicks. Using a paring knife, he slices them open to inspect their construction, noting that the Hicks backpack contains a wavy hardboard lining. Poirot calls Inspector Sharpe to request the specific dates and times of past police investigations on Chestnut Street. Sharpe provides three instances: an investigation into seditious pamphlets on December 18th, an investigation into Montague Jones on February 24th, and an investigation into William Robinson on March 6th. Poirot concludes the call and reviews a revised list of items provided by Mrs. Habberton, which includes various personal belongings such as a stethoscope, a diamond ring, and boric acid.
